seo.page_title: Land Rover Discovery HSE import NZ
seo.meta_description: Land Rover Discovery HSE import NZ. Seven-seat space, strong petrol performance and clear landed pricing from Japan.
seo.h1_tag: Land Rover Discovery HSE import NZ
seo.og_title: Land Rover Discovery HSE import NZ
seo.og_description: Import a 2018 Land Rover Discovery HSE from Japan with clear landed pricing, auction documents before bidding, and NZ-based support since 2009.
seo.primary_keyword: Land Rover Discovery HSE import NZ
secondary_keywords: Land Rover Discovery Japan import NZ

blocks.hero_h1: Land Rover Discovery HSE import NZ
blocks.hero_subtitle: This Land Rover Discovery HSE looks right in a dark metallic or silver factory colour, which tends to suit the shape and hides day-to-day dust better than black. HSE is one of the better-equipped Discovery grades, so it appeals to buyers who want the full seven-seat SUV experience rather than just the badge.
proof_points: Transparent pricing, compliance is part of the process
blocks.why_kiwis_buy: People usually buy a Discovery HSE because it covers a lot of jobs at once. You get proper family space, a commanding driving position and the sort of towing and touring ability that makes sense for long NZ trips, but you also need to go in knowing it will cost more to run than a Highlander or CX-8.

savings_block.headline: Where the money goes, from Japan auction to NZ landed cost
savings_block.body: With this model, the final number depends heavily on year, kilometres, condition and spec, so we quote each car individually rather than guessing a broad range. The landed figure is made up of the Japanese auction purchase price, inland transport in Japan, shipping, marine insurance, NZ port costs, entry certification, compliance and our fee, so you can see where the money is going before you commit.

cta_form.headline: Find your Discovery HSE - we'll do the searching
cta_form.subtext: Fill in your details to get started. We'll search the Japanese auctions and come back with real options and pricing by email.

blocks.video_caption: A walkaround video is useful on a Discovery because you can usually spot the seat layout, tailgate setup and whether it has the larger infotainment screen. On this kind of SUV, those details matter more than brochure talk.
blocks.main_usp_deep: The main draw with a Discovery HSE is breadth of ability. It gives you seven-seat practicality, a proper high-riding SUV driving position and the sort of long-trip comfort that makes sense if you regularly carry people and gear. The HSE grade is also where the Discovery starts to feel properly well equipped, with the comfort and driver-assist features most buyers actually ask for. One useful thing to know is that Japanese-market examples are often bought as family or executive transport rather than hard off-road use, which can make them worth a close look when the auction notes are clean.

buyer_persona.headline: Who is this car right for?
buyer_persona.body: This one works well for buyers who need one vehicle to cover family duties, road trips and occasional towing without stepping into a commercial-style wagon. It also suits people who value the upright seating position and quieter cabin feel of a larger premium SUV, and who are realistic about servicing and running costs. If that sounds like you, it is a solid match.

trust_company.headline: Why import with ImportYourCar.co.nz?
trust_company.body: We have been importing cars for NZ buyers since 2009 and we specialise in direct Japanese auction sourcing. Before bidding, we provide the auction sheet, a translation of the notes and a clear cost breakdown so you can make the call with the paperwork in front of you. We are an NZ-based team, and once the car arrives we guide it through entry certification and compliance as part of the process.

car_specs_benefits.benefit: Usually the point in the range where the Discovery gets the comfort and driver-assist features most NZ buyers want.
Smoother and quieter around town and on long trips than many diesel alternatives, especially for lighter towing and family use.
Useful if you need occasional third-row seating without moving into a van.
Gives you access to documented examples with auction notes and odometer records before bidding.
A later-shape Discovery that still feels current in design and cabin layout.

standout_features_block.headline: The clever bits
standout_features_block.intro: On a Discovery HSE, the worthwhile features are the ones that make a large SUV easier to place, load and live with.

items.title: Terrain Response
items.body: Terrain Response is one of the Discovery's key systems because it changes throttle, gearbox and traction settings to suit the surface underneath you. That matters in NZ because a big family SUV often ends up doing more than sealed-road commuting, even if it is mostly used as a daily driver.
items.image_hint: Centre console drive mode selector with terrain graphics visible on the screen

items.title: Power tailgate
items.body: A power tailgate is more useful on a Discovery than on a smaller SUV because the opening is large and the load height matters when you are dealing with bags, sports gear or a pram. It is one of those features buyers appreciate every week rather than once a year.
items.image_hint: Rear of the Discovery with the tailgate open and luggage area visible

items.title: Surround-view camera system
items.body: Large SUVs live or die by visibility in car parks, and a camera system makes a real difference when placing the Discovery in tighter spaces. It is also handy when checking kerbs, towball alignment or backing into a narrow driveway.
items.image_hint: Infotainment screen showing a parking or surround camera view

items.title: Driver-assist package
items.body: Higher-spec Discovery grades commonly include adaptive cruise and lane-support functions that take some of the effort out of longer motorway runs. On a vehicle built for covering distance, that is the sort of technology worth paying attention to.
items.image_hint: Steering wheel controls and instrument display showing cruise or lane-assist information

tech_deep_dive_block.headline: What is actually fitted
tech_deep_dive_block.intro: The Discovery makes more sense when you look at the systems that shape how it drives and how easy it is to use.

items.title: HSE trim
items.body: HSE stands for High Specification Equipment, and in Discovery terms it is the grade many buyers target because it usually brings the cabin and convenience features that suit a premium family SUV. Rather than paying for the badge alone, you are generally stepping into the part of the range where the seats, infotainment and driver-assist equipment feel complete. That matters on an import because trim level can change the ownership experience more than the odometer does.

items.title: Terrain Response four-wheel-drive control
items.body: Terrain Response is Land Rover's drive management system for changing how the vehicle responds to different surfaces. It adjusts the drivetrain and traction settings to suit conditions, which helps a heavy SUV feel more composed when the road turns loose or slippery. Even if the car spends most of its life on sealed roads, the system is part of why a Discovery feels more sure-footed than a soft-roader.

items.title: Air suspension on equipped vehicles
items.body: Many Discovery HSE examples are fitted with air suspension, and it is one of the reasons these drive with that settled long-distance feel. It can also help with access height and load levelling, which is useful when passengers or luggage vary from trip to trip. The flip side is that it is a system worth checking carefully through auction notes, service history and later NZ inspection because complexity is part of the ownership picture.

cta_mid_page.headline: Want us to look for the right spec?
cta_mid_page.subtext: Send us your brief and we will come back with auction options and a clear landed estimate.

models_comparison.intro: With Discovery HSE imports, the useful differences are usually year, equipment level and how well the vehicle has been maintained.

rows.notes: 2018 is a later-shape model and generally sits in the safer zone for modern safety and emissions expectations, though each vehicle still needs to be checked on its own paperwork.
This page is focused on the petrol Discovery HSE, chosen more for refinement and smoothness than low running costs.
HSE usually sits above lower grades and is the one many buyers want for the better comfort, camera and driver-assist equipment.

Later petrol SUVs like this typically meet NZ entry standards if documentation is clean and no modifications are noted, but each vehicle is assessed individually during entry certification and compliance.
Discovery HSE examples do appear in Japan, but not in the same volume as mainstream Japanese SUVs, so colour, kilometres and option choices can affect wait time.

steps.title: Tell us what you're after
steps.body: Fill in the form or message us. We confirm your brief and give you a cost breakdown before anything moves forward.

steps.title: We search the auctions
steps.body: We monitor USS, TAA, HAA and others. For Discovery HSE listings, we pay close attention to grade notes, service-book references and any mention of suspension, electronics or modification history on the auction sheet.

steps.title: You approve, we bid
steps.body: We send you the auction sheet and our recommendation. Once you're happy, you pay a deposit and we bid on your behalf.

steps.title: Shipping to NZ
steps.body: Typically 2-4 weeks depending on the sailing schedule. For less common models, allow a little extra time once the auction clears. We keep you updated along the way.

steps.title: Compliance and delivery
steps.body: The car goes through NZ entry certification and compliance. Once it's through, we arrange delivery or you can collect.

risks_solutions.intro: Importing a Discovery HSE is straightforward when the paperwork and model history are handled properly from the start.

items.risk: Not knowing which HSE spec you are actually bidding on
items.solution: We translate the auction sheet, confirm the listed grade and explain what is noted so you know whether the car matches your brief.

items.risk: Hidden costs in the landed figure
items.solution: We break down the Japanese purchase price, shipping, insurance, NZ port costs, entry certification, compliance and our fee before you commit.

items.risk: Unclear auction grading and condition notes
items.solution: We explain the auction grade, damage codes, interior notes and odometer paperwork in plain language so you are not guessing from a screenshot.

blocks.how_to_use_iyc: You tell us the Discovery HSE spec, budget and must-have features. We search the Japanese auctions, send you the paperwork and pricing, then handle the buying and shipping if you want to proceed. If you are still comparing options, we can start with a rough landed estimate first.

faq_block.q: Is the Land Rover Discovery HSE a cheap SUV to own?
faq_block.a: No, not really. It suits buyers who want the space, capability and premium feel, and who are comfortable with higher servicing and parts costs than a mainstream Japanese SUV.

faq_block.q: What makes up the landed cost?
faq_block.a: The landed figure usually includes the auction purchase price, Japan-side transport, shipping, insurance, NZ port charges, entry certification, compliance and our fee. We set that out clearly before bidding.

faq_block.q: Will a 2018 Discovery HSE usually meet NZ standards?
faq_block.a: Usually, later-model examples like this typically meet NZ entry standards if documentation is clean and no modifications are noted. Final assessment still happens in NZ during entry certification and compliance.

faq_block.q: How long does it take to get one here?
faq_block.a: Once bought, shipping is typically around 2 to 4 weeks depending on vessel timing. Allow extra time for finding the right car, port processing and NZ compliance.

faq_block.q: How do I know what condition the auction car is in?
faq_block.a: We provide the auction sheet, a translation of the notes and an explanation of the grade and damage markings. That gives you a documented picture before any bid is placed.

footer_cta.headline: Ready to price a Discovery HSE from Japan?
footer_cta.subtext: Send us the spec you want and we will come back with real auction options, paperwork and a clear landed estimate.

subscription_block.headline: Stay in the loop
subscription_block.subtext: Get auction alerts, fresh stock updates and practical import guides by email.

blocks.company_positioning: ImportYourCar.co.nz is an NZ-based Japanese vehicle import company operating since 2009. We specialise in sourcing vehicles from Japanese auctions, translating the paperwork and managing the process through to arrival in New Zealand.

landed_cost_breakdown.intro: Your landed cost is the full path from Japan to NZ, not just the hammer price.

rows.notes: Varies with year, kilometres, colour, grade and option level.
Includes transport from Japan and marine insurance during the voyage.
Handled once the vehicle arrives, with final outcomes depending on the vehicle and its documentation.

auction_checklist.check: Confirm the exact Discovery grade and trim listed on the auction sheet
auction_checklist.why: This tells you whether the car is genuinely an HSE and helps avoid paying HSE money for a lower-spec vehicle.

auction_checklist.check: Translate every damage code and interior note
auction_checklist.why: You get a clearer picture of cosmetic wear and how the vehicle has been looked after.
This matters for entry certification, future servicing and whether the vehicle still suits your brief.

auction_checklist.check: Check the odometer certificate and service-book references
auction_checklist.why: That helps verify the recorded kilometres and whether there is documented maintenance history.

not_for_you_block.headline: Who this may not suit
items: If your main priority is low running costs, a Discovery HSE is probably not the right place to start.
If you rarely need seven seats or towing ability, a smaller SUV may make more financial sense.
If you want the simplest possible ownership experience, a mainstream Japanese SUV is often easier to budget for.

alternatives_block.intro: There are times when another model may suit the job better.

rows.better_if: You want seven seats with lower ownership costs and easier parts support.
rows.tradeoff: You give up some of the Discovery's cabin feel, off-road hardware and sense of occasion.

rows.better_if: You want a family SUV that is easier to park and generally lighter on servicing costs.
rows.tradeoff: It does not offer the same heavy-duty SUV character or the same towing-focused appeal.

engine_drivetrain_block.headline: Engine and drivetrain
engine_drivetrain_block.body: The petrol Discovery HSE is bought more for refinement and smooth power delivery than for low running costs. In a vehicle this size, that matters, because a smoother petrol setup can make town driving, motorway merging and long-distance touring feel easier and quieter. The Discovery's four-wheel-drive hardware is also a big part of the appeal, giving it a more serious drivetrain layout than many crossover SUVs. If you are comparing it with softer seven-seat options, this is one of the reasons it feels like a proper large SUV rather than just a tall wagon.
checklist: Petrol power suits buyers who value smoothness over minimum running costs
Four-wheel-drive setup is part of the Discovery's core appeal
Best matched to mixed family, touring and towing use
